title: salix phylicifolia var. hibernica
intro: Salix phylicifolia var. hibernica, also known as Irish alder, is a variety of the willow tree that is native to Ireland and other parts of Europe. This deciduous shrub or small tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the salicylic acid content in its bark. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia var. hibernica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the bark in hot water to create a tea, or by using the bark to make a salve or tincture for topical application.
